rules.rationale: Licensed physicians in New Jersey may prescribe within the ordinary scope of medical practice.
rules.citation_title: New Jersey Board of Medical Examiners

rules.limitations: Psychologists in New Jersey do not hold independent prescriptive authority.
rules.rationale: Psychologist licensure does not include prescribing authority in New Jersey.
rules.citation_title: New Jersey Board of Psychological Examiners

rules.limitations: Advanced practice nurses may prescribe subject to New Jersey collaborative and formulary requirements.
rules.rationale: New Jersey APNs have prescriptive authority under state nursing rules, but it is not title-only and should be surfaced as limited unless the source clarifies the prescriber role.

rules.limitations: Physician assistants prescribe under delegated physician supervision and documented scope.
rules.rationale: New Jersey physician assistant prescribing is delegated and should remain limited unless directly evidenced.
rules.citation_title: New Jersey Physician Assistant Advisory Committee

rules.limitations: Clinical social worker licensure does not carry prescriptive authority.
rules.rationale: Behavioral health licensure alone does not imply prescribing authority.
